context.editDescription: Three prop additions (editor-only changes, no PHP changes): openOnImageClick={false} and inlineSVG={true} added to FooterInfo.tsx logo ImageDropUploader; openOnImageClick={false} added to FooterInfoSocialItem.tsx social icon ImageDropUploader

html.message: No previous HTML snapshot found — first run

contentComparison.reason: First snapshot or code modified — running full QA

build.notes: bun run build and lint both pass. Pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in unrelated blocks (Accordion, Archive, ContentWithMedia, FullWidthMedia, PostHero, Tabs) — not introduced by this change.

lint.warnings: Pre-existing warnings in unrelated blocks only. No warnings in Footer block files.

registration.notes: block.json schema valid, naming correct (takt/footer-*), all file references exist, attribute types valid.

tsxPhpSync.notes: No PHP changes were needed or made. PHP already uses theme_output_svg_or_img() for both logo (FooterInfo.php line 35) and social icons (FooterInfoSocialItem.php line 37), which handles inline SVG output natively on the frontend. The new props (openOnImageClick, inlineSVG) are editor-only ImageDropUploader props with no frontend PHP equivalent needed. Automated sync check passes with only a known false-positive warning: root element mismatch (TSX shows <inspectorcontrols> as outermost JSX node but actual rendered wrapper is <section> via useBlockProps — this is standard Gutenberg editor pattern, not a real sync issue).
tsxPhpSync.falsePositiveWarning: Root wrapper element mismatch: TSX uses <inspectorcontrols>, PHP uses <section> — known false positive for blocks using InspectorControls as JSX sibling

testPage.notes: 6 takt/footer instances found, all wrapped in takt/demo-container. Block structure verified including footer-info-social-item children.

notes: 7 wp-block-takt-footer-info sections rendered across 6 footer instances (one instance has two footer-info rows).
Logo homepage links (a[aria-label='Go to homepage']) found in sections 0 and 6.

Section 6 confirms SVG inline rendering: svg_in_logo selector found (count: 1). The inlineSVG={true} prop on the editor's ImageDropUploader correctly corresponds to theme_output_svg_or_img() on the PHP side which inlines SVGs.
Section 0 renders an tag for logo (PNG/non-SVG image) — correct fallback behavior.
Social items (li.wp-block-takt-footer-info-social-item) render correctly: 5 items in sections 0/2/6, 1 item in section 4, 2 items in section 5.
Social links have accessible text (link titles used as sr-only span, aria-label when set).
Sections 1 and 3 are minimal/empty footer-info instances — render without errors.

edgeCases.notes: Logo with PNG renders as (correct fallback). Logo with SVG renders inline (verified in section 6). Footer-info without a logo renders without errors (sections 1-5 without logo link). Social items without logoId show ImageDropUploader placeholder in editor only; PHP correctly guards with if (!empty($logoId)). Empty footer-info section (section 3) renders without errors.

notes: Logo link has aria-label='Go to homepage' for icon-only link (no text).
Social links have sr-only span with link title for screen readers, plus optional aria-label from label attribute.
Social links without accessible name fallback to 'Social media link' in PHP (FooterInfoSocialItem.php line 25-26).

passedChecks: Lint passes with no Footer-specific warnings
openOnImageClick={false} correctly added to FooterInfo.tsx logo ImageDropUploader (line 88)
inlineSVG={true} correctly added to FooterInfo.tsx logo ImageDropUploader (line 89)
openOnImageClick={false} correctly added to FooterInfoSocialItem.tsx ImageDropUploader (line 237)
No PHP changes needed — theme_output_svg_or_img() already handles SVG inline output
TSX/PHP sync validated — automated check passes
Test page accessible with 6 footer instances in demo containers
Rendered output correct — logo renders as SVG inline or img fallback, social items render with accessible links
Empty/minimal footer-info instances render without errors
